My cab driver: “What conference is it?”
Me: “It’s a tech conference called KubeCon.”

Him: [bursts out laughing]
Me: 😅
Him: 🤣🤣🤣
Me: 🫠

Hello KubeCon! I’m speaking today on building extensible platforms and I can’t wait. And for those of you building platform shaped projects… I have a challenge for you, if you’re feeling ambitious 👀

As always, please feel free to say hi if you see me!

Hello #KubeCon day three! (Or two? Or four? Five?? Counting is hard)

The famous Kubernetes dress returns, too. Catch me floating around if you can!

🚨 ingress-nginx is now retired 🚨 

Stop by the SIG Security booth P-4B at #kubecon #cloudnativecon for a commemorative sticker!

We will be there this afternoon and Thursday afternoon. Hope to see you there!

Very impressed with the ventilation in the #kubecon public areas; the CO2 is usually in the 450-500 range; 435 in the press room with 10 people in here, which is about the same as being outside. Hope the session and lunch rooms and especially the parties are like that

So Kubecon EU is fucking ENORMOUS. I will report on numbers later but first impressions are just that it's simply very big. The @cncf.io is evidently still a critical foundation (pun intended) for modern software workloads.
Just landed at SLC for Kubecon. Where’s everybody at?

I’m sad to be missing #KubeCon this week. It looks like it is going well and I wish I was there!

I am, however, heading up to Vancouver on Thursday for #AtmosphereConf. Looking forward to learning a ton and meeting new people. If you see me please say hi!

Currently at the biggest KubeCon event yet. Meanwhile, I wrote some reflections on an entirely different conference from last week, and why it was so special #MonkiGras2026: diginomica.com/monkigras-20...

yeah this one's not great, tbh. litellm is pretty core infrastructure.

She go to KubeCon day two. No more talks from me, but that just means I have all day to wander around and say hi to everyone!

😭😭😭🙏 I got so many compliments about the talk. And the most important validation came from the most active maintainer on the project.

The #PlatformEngineeringDay greatness at the #KubeCon #CloudNativeCon doesn't stop: now we got @hazelweakly.me talking about building extensible platforms!

Things usually go this way: Strong components → system → complexity → components → ... Is the cycle unavoidable?

I wish there were more "outsider guides" to technologies, that explained what it is for people who hear about it or are adjacent to it but don't need to use it. Like what is Kubernetes? What problems does it solve? What makes it a hard problem to solve? What are the legendary warts?

We're at KubeCon Amsterdam. 

If you want to find out how to prevent misconfigurations ending up in production, come by (Hall 5, 1163).

Or join us for a chat about configuration, preventable outages, schemas, or our configuration control plane.

It’s the last day at #KubeCon Europe!

But hell yeah for the community standards!

That had to be the most insightful keynote so far - but I'm biased toward platform topics haha
Go go @abangser.bsky.social for a #KubeCon #CloudNativeCon keynote on platform engineering - was really looking forward to it!

KubeCon EU is live and the agent talks are everywhere. My favorite pattern emerging: agents that negotiate with infrastructure instead of just deploying to it. The platform isn't a destination anymore — it's a conversation partner. That's the real shift.

recent_posts.text:
It’s Abby Bangser on the #KubeCon Keynote stage! 

➡️ Find the bottleneck, there always is one
➡️ AI has changed the bottleneck 
➡️ Get the right tools into the hands of your builders (and they have AI Agents now)
➡️ Agents can only move as fast as their platform

Another great KubeCon talk from @abangser.bsky.social  imploring the Kubernetes people to remember how important platforms are: they make all of his stuff usable for developers and valuable to the organizations that run it all. Without a platform, you’re just put another bottleneck in place.

Supply chain attack hits litellm (95M downloads).
Backdoor runs on import + every Python startup.
Steals creds, spreads via Kubernetes, persists silently.
Same campaign hitting multiple ecosystems.
Dev tools = new attack surface?
Follow us for more updates.
#CyberSecurity #Infosec #OpenSource #PyPI

recent_posts.text:
AI is moving the bottleneck in software teams from writing code to reviewing it.

If 50% of AI-generated patches that pass tests get rejected by maintainers, the scarce resource isn't coding speed; it's judgment.

Wrote a deep analysis on what this means for systems engineers 🧵
#Kubernetes #kubecon

KubeCon Amsterdam right now and the biggest theme isn't containers — it's AI workload orchestration on K8s.

The infra layer is eating the AI layer. Again.

Five years ago: 'K8s is just for microservices.'
Now: 'K8s is the control plane for everything.'

The boring stuff always wins.

recent_posts.text:
unpopular opinion: kubernetes is fine but most teams adopted it because it sounded serious, not because they needed it

docker compose handles 90% of production workloads under 10k req/day

scale the complexity when you actually have the traffic

Which is more active and more authentic at #kubecon, X or Bluesky? I asked Claude for analysis of last two days. Across both, 2/3rd of posts are corporate accounts or humans promoting, with 1/3rd being genuine experiences and shared learnings. X has more posts, but Bluesky has more "real humans"

The best way to start the day is with a group breakfast.

CNCF Ambassadors and Kubestronauts gather for a morning of collaboration. If you see them around the RAI, say hello—or find them around the Network Nook to chat!
